description: Create a content slide on the Collingridge Dilemma with a sidebar-right layout, showing a custom process/diagram of innovation governance over time and explanatory bullet points on the right.

header.headline: Future implications of technological advances regarding humanity are not entirely foreseeable – Regulators face a double-bind dilemma
header.sub: Innovation governance suffers from the Collingridge Dilemma

main.title: Collingridge Dilemma diagram
main.body: A conceptual governance diagram plots control over technology against time after introduction. Early in a technology’s life, control is high but impacts are hard to predict; later, impacts become clearer but control is lower. The slide emphasizes that effective innovation governance requires both foresight and adaptability. Key labels in the visual include collaboration, standards, regulations, rules, and practices; a central callout highlights the challenge of regulation under uncertainty. The governance box conveys that regulators must act when consequences are uncertain, while later intervention becomes harder as systems become entrenched.

items.text: The Collingridge Dilemma states that regulating innovation is difficult because social consequences cannot be predicted early on.
Once harmful consequences become visible, the technology is often already embedded and costly to change.
Regulators therefore face a double bind: intervene too early with limited evidence or too late when control is reduced.
Governance responses span collaboration, standards, regulations, rules, and evolving practices.
